name: Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (size undisclosed) served as 4 parallel specialized agent roles (coordinator 'Grok/Captain', research/fact-check 'Harper', math/logic 'Benjamin', creative/balance 'Lucas') sharing weights and KV-cache, debating before synthesizing one final answer
note: Multi-agent sibling of Grok 4.20, paralleling how 'Grok 4 Heavy' relates to 'Grok-4' elsewhere in this corpus: 4 specialized agent roles collaborate at inference time on every complex query, running as parallel replicas of the same underlying model rather than 4 independent models. xAI states the marginal inference cost is roughly 1.5-2.5x a single pass, not 4x. Public beta Feb 17, 2026; full API GA Mar 10, 2026 alongside the base model.
verificationNotes:
Sibling entry to 'Grok 4.20', confirmed via the same primary source: docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes explicitly announces both together ('Mar 10 -- Grok 4.20 and Grok 4.20 Multi-agent are live'), and I separately fetched the dedicated model card at docs.x.ai/developers/models/grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta-0309 directly (model slug `grok-4.20-multi-agent-0309`; aliases: grok-4.20-multi-agent, grok-4.20-multi-agent-latest, grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta-0309, grok-4.20-multi-agent-experimental-beta-0304). Same pricing as the base model ($1.25/$2.50 per 1M, $0.20 cached) and same stated 1,000,000-token context window, subject to the identical primary-vs-aggregator discrepancy documented on the sibling 'Grok 4.20' entry (independent aggregators including LLM Stats -- which separately tracks 'Grok-4.20 Multi-Agent Beta', released Mar 9, 2026, at $2.00/$6.00 and 2M context -- again disagree with the primary source's 1M/$1.25 figures, likely reflecting a later point-release; I trusted the live primary-source page). The 4-agent architecture and named roles (Grok/Captain, Harper, Benjamin, Lucas) are independently corroborated across multiple unrelated secondary write-ups (natural20.com citing NextBigFuture and a Yahoo Finance piece on Alpha Arena live-trading results; aimlapi.com; cometapi.com) describing the same four agents consistently enough to treat as accurate, even though xAI itself never published an official blog post for this release (confirmed absent: x.ai/news/grok-4-20 404s, and natural20.com explicitly notes xAI's last x.ai/news entry at the time was the Nov 2025 Grok 4.1 post). NO VERIFIED BENCHMARK SCORES for the same reasons documented on the sibling 'Grok 4.20' entry: no xAI-published benchmark table exists, LLM Stats shows 'Score pending' for this exact model, and I deliberately did not use the single-sourced Alpha Arena live-trading returns (+34.59%) or an 'estimated LMArena Elo of 1505-1535' cited by natural20.com, since neither is a standard benchmark in this corpus's approved slug taxonomy and the Elo figure is explicitly self-described as an estimate rather than a measured result. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): Final widened-methodology search for Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent (corpus/models/spacexai/grok-4-20-multi-agent.json) found NO citable benchmark result and confirms the conclusion should stand as "no public data exists." Not applicable for Chinese/Japanese-vendor search angle — this is xAI/SpaceXAI, an English-language vendor, so steps 1-2 of the brief don't apply. Checked per step 3/4:

- Read existing verificationNotes first: prior research already established no xAI-published benchmark table exists for this SKU, LLM Stats showed "Score pending," and an Alpha Arena live-trading return + a self-described "estimated" LMArena Elo (1505-1535) were deliberately excluded as non-standard/non-measured.
- Re-fetched llm-stats.com/models/grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta-0309 directly: still shows pricing/context only, zero benchmark scores.
- Fetched artificialanalysis.ai/providers/xai (full xAI model roster with Intelligence Index scores): no "Multi-Agent" SKU listed at all — only base Grok 4.20 0309 / 0309 v2 (reasoning and non-reasoning) appear. Confirmed AA tracks the base model only, not this variant.
- Fetched llm-stats.com/benchmarks/lmarena-text: only Grok-4.1 and Grok-4.1 Thinking listed for xAI; no Grok 4.20 of any kind, let alone the multi-agent SKU.
- Fetched openrouter.ai/x-ai/grok-4.20-multi-agent: pricing/context/provider info only, no benchmark scores.
- Fetched benchlm.ai/compare/grok-4-20-beta-vs-grok-4-20-multi-agent-beta (a dedicated aggregator comparison page): explicitly states "BenchLM does not have sourced benchmark coverage for Grok 4.20 Multi-agent yet," all 19 tested benchmark columns show "—" for this model, "0 shared benchmark results."
- Fetched apertis.ai/models/grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta: no benchmark scores present.
- Fetched aimlapi.com's Grok 4.20 review blog: confirmed all its reported numbers (Arena Elo ~1493, GPQA ~88%, HLE 50.7%) are attributed to "Grok 4.20" generically, with the multi-agent SKU only named as one of three release variants, no scores isolated to it.
- Web-searched combinations of "Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent" against GAIA, tau-bench, BFCL, function-calling/tool-use benchmarks (relevant given the model's agentic multi-agent design) — found general discussion of what these benchmarks measure but zero scores tied to this specific model.
- Checked for an xAI blog/Wayback angle: x.ai/news has no dedicated post (confirmed absent in both this and prior rounds); no distinct Wayback snapshot surfaced with different data.

Verdict: every number in circulation for "Grok 4.20" in mid-2026 web content is attributed to the base model, not the "-multi-agent-0309" SKU specifically, and the one aggregator (BenchLM) that explicitly tracks per-model sourced coverage confirms zero benchmark data exists for this variant. This looks like a genuine case of a vendor never publishing standard benchmark results broken out for a model configuration (the multi-agent inference mode is treated by both xAI and third parties as an inference-time architecture choice layered on the same base model, not a separately benchmarked release). Recommend closing out further benchmark-hunting for this entry unless a future xAI blog post or aggregator specifically isolates multi-agent-mode scores. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): ROUND-8 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15) for Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent (corpus/models/spacexai/grok-4-20-multi-agent.json). Read existing verificationNotes first (7 prior rounds documented, including a Round-7 exhaustive sweep of LLM Stats, Artificial Analysis, OpenRouter, BenchLM, Apertis, aimlapi — all concluding zero benchmark data exists for this SKU). This round checked the two specific structured sources requested, plus a quick PapersWithCode pass:

1. Epoch AI (epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json and .csv, fetched directly, ~180KB JSON / 5981-row CSV): grepped both files exhaustively for every form of the model name ("grok", "4.20", "multi-agent", "spacex"). Epoch AI DOES track the base model under the exact id `grok-4.20-0309-reasoning` (display name "Grok 4.20", released 2026-02-17) with real scored rows for GPQA diamond (89.33%), Chess Puzzles (24%), SimpleQA Verified (35.1%), OTIS M... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] ROUND (2026-07-16), targeted at the two specific source types requested, found NO new benchmark data for Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent (corpus/models/spacexai/grok-4-20-multi-agent.json). Read existing verificationNotes first — 13+ prior rounds already exhausted general web search, native-language/regional leaderboards, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LLM Stats, Artificial Analysis, OpenRouter, BenchLM, Apertis, aimlapi, all concluding zero benchmark data exists for this SKU distinct from the base Grok 4.20 model.

1) HuggingFace model card check — NEGATIVE, and inapplicable in principle: this model has NO HuggingFace repo. xAI does host older open-weight releases on HF (xai-org/grok-1, xai-org/grok-2), but Grok 4.x is a closed, API-only, proprietary model line with no published weights, so there is no HF model card to fetch for "grok-4.20-multi-agent" under any org (xai-org, x-ai, spacexai, etc.). Web searches for "grok-4.20-multi-agent huggingface", "huggingface.co xai grok-4.20", and site:huggingface.co queries returned zero matching repos — only unrelated Grok-1/Grok-2 pages and a third-party CometAPI/grok4 mirror repo. I fetched that CometAPI/grok4 README.md directly (https://huggingface.co/CometAPI/grok4/blob/main/README.md): it covers base "Grok 4" and "Grok 4 Code" only, lists AIME 98.8 / GPQA 88 / SWE-Bench 75 / HLE 45% as a plain bulleted list (no formal eval-results table, no eval_results.json in Files-and-versions), and does not mention "Grok 4.20" or any Multi-Agent variant at all. So: model confirmed to have no HF presence.

2) Semantic Scholar / academic citation search — NEGATIVE, and also inapplicable in principle: unlike ERNIE 3.0 Titan (arXiv 2112.12731), Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent has no underlying arXiv paper or technical report to be cited by — it is a commercial product/API release announced via xAI's docs.x.ai release notes, not a research publication. Confirmed by web search for "arxiv.org Grok 4.20 xAI technical report": only found xAI's PDF model cards for Grok 4 (data.x.ai/2025-08-20-grok-4-model-card.pdf) and Grok 4.1 (data.x.ai/2025-11-17-grok-4-1-model-card.pdf) — no Grok 4.20 or Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent model card exists at that domain, and no arXiv entry exists for either. I queried the Semantic Scholar Graph API directly (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search); it was heavily rate-limited (repeated HTTP 429) but one unquoted query for "Grok 4.20" did succeed and returned 17,124 total hits, all generic LLM-comparison papers (medical/education/engineering domains comparing ChatGPT/Gemini/Grok/DeepSeek) with no paper specifically discussing "Grok 4.20" the version or the Multi-Agent architecture — none in the visible results reported a benchmark score attributable to this specific SKU. Exact-phrase queries ("Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent") consistently 429'd despite multiple retries with backoff (3s/8s/10s/15s/20s waits); given zero underlying paper exists to be cited in the first place, further retries are unlikely to surface anything even if the rate limit clears. Also checked semanticscholar.org's own search UI directly — it's client-side rendered and returned no results via fetch (expected, not informative either way), and cross-checked with plain web search for "Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent" + arxiv/scholar terms, which surfaced only vendor/aggregator blog coverage (BenchLM, natural20, aimlapi, designforonline, verdent.ai, etc.) already catalogued in prior rounds, no academic citations.

Net verdict: both requested source types are structurally inapplicable to this entry — no HF repo exists (closed proprietary model), no academic paper exists to attract citations (commercial release, not a publication). This corroborates rather than contradicts the prior 13 rounds' conclusion that no citable standard benchmark score exists for this specific SKU in the corpus's approved taxonomy. Recommend treating "zero results" as a stable, confirmed state for this model going forward absent a future xAI blog post or the model's inclusion in a real academic evaluation paper.

ROUND-21 LIGHT DELTA-CHECK (2026-07-16, same-day as Round-20): Per instructions, did NOT repeat the full exhaustive search (20 prior rounds already exhausted xAI primary sources, LLM Stats, Artificial Analysis, OpenRouter, Vals.ai, Epoch AI, BenchLM, Apertis, aimlapi, HuggingFace, and academic/arXiv search). Re-checked only for genuinely new information: re-fetched llm-stats.com/models/grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta-0309 (still "Score pending," no benchmark data populated), artificialanalysis.ai/providers/xai (11 SKUs listed for xAI, none labeled "Multi-Agent" — only base "Grok 4.20 0309 v2" reasoning/non-reasoning variants), and openrouter.ai/x-ai/grok-4.20-multi-agent (pricing/context only, explicitly no GPQA/MMLU/HLE/coding scores). Re-checked vals.ai and epoch.ai via search: vals.ai tracks only "Grok 4.20 (Reasoning)" (grok_grok-4.20-0309-reasoning, Vals Index 39.11%) with no multi-agent-specific entry; epoch.ai's indexed pages have no "Grok 4.20" model page at all (only Grok 4, Grok 4 Heavy, Grok 4 Fast, Grok 3). One new finding worth recording: a content-aggregator site, designforonline.com, now has two separate pages for this exact model that directly contradict each other — /ai-models/xai-grok-4-20-multi-agent-beta/ lists specific numbers (GPQA Diamond 88.5%, HLE 30%, SciCode 44.7%, TerminalBench Hard 40.9%, tau2-Bench 96.5%, IFBench 82.9%) claiming attribution to "Artificial Analysis and Hugging Face," while /ai-models/xai-grok-4-20-multi-agent/ for the same model explicitly states "this specific variant has no benchmark data of its own" and shows only a self-admitted "editorially curated" composite score. I verified directly against Artificial Analysis's own xAI provider page and found none of those specific numbers or a Multi-Agent SKU present at all, confirming designforonline.com fabricates/interpolates figures inconsistently even across its own pages for the same model. Recommend excluding designforonline.com as a source for this entry going forward. Verdict unchanged: zero-result state remains confirmed and stable; no new credible evidence found since Round 20.
